evidence: VARC achieves 60.4% accuracy, outperforming scratch-trained methods and competitive with LLMs, suggesting visual learning enables abstraction.
ARC concepts are inherently visual (reflection, symmetry, gravity), motivating a vision-centric approach where abstraction arises from visual learning.
Humans solve ARC by reasoning through common sense from the visual world, making vision-driven approaches natural for abstraction.
VARC (18M params) achieves 54.5% accuracy, surpassing recurrent methods, indicating visual learning enables abstraction without language.
Ensembled VARC achieves 60.4% accuracy, matching human performance, supporting that visual learning enables abstraction.
U-Net variants achieve decent accuracy, suggesting this problem can also be effectively addressed by classical vision backbones.
Extending from 1D to 2D positional embedding is beneficial, supporting that explicit 2D positional modeling is essential.
2D ROPE improves accuracy, observed as beneficial for 2D positional embedding, supporting its essential nature.
Replacing 2D ROPE with 1D ROPE degrades accuracy by 3.5 points, demonstrating 2D positional modeling is essential.
2x2 patchification leads to a 2.4 point gain, as each patch covers multiple colors, enriching the data space for learning.
Fully flexible translation augmentation on the canvas yields an additional 2.9 points gain.
Translation augmentation yields a 2.9 point gain; these augmentations encourage learning invariance to geometric transformations.
Scale augmentation enabled by the canvas yields a substantial 6.2 points gain.
Scale augmentation yields a substantial 6.2 points gain; these augmentations encourage learning invariance to geometric transformations.
Scale augmentation yields a substantial 6.2 points gain, explaining why it's substantial due to ViT's lack of scale invariance bias.
Offline training greatly improves TTT performance, suggesting that common sense about the visual world can be learned from the training set.
Even without offline training, TTT achieves nontrivial accuracy (26.4%), suggesting some tasks can be solved tabula rasa.
Independent TTT performs better than joint TTT, even though joint relies on a stronger assumption about multiple test tasks at once.
Multi-view inference boosts pass@1 accuracy by 13.9 points; this large gain is explained by single pixel errors invalidating ARC predictions.
VARC (18M params) achieves 54.5% accuracy on ARC-1, supporting that visual learning enables abstraction without language.
Ensembled VARC achieves 60.4% accuracy on ARC-1, supporting that visual learning enables abstraction without language.
VARC (ensemble) matches average human performance on ARC-1, closing the gap and supporting visual learning for abstraction.
VARC substantially outperforms recurrent models trained from scratch, indicating visual learning enables abstraction without language.
VARC is competitive with many popular LLM-based methods, supporting that visual learning enables abstraction without language.
VARC uses a model several orders of magnitude smaller than LLMs, yet achieves competitive results, supporting visual learning's efficiency for abstraction.
VARC does not rely on internet-scale pre-training data, yet achieves competitive results, supporting visual learning's effectiveness for abstraction.
Attention maps show our model correctly reasons about pixel-to-pixel relationships.
Different layers exhibit different specialties, such as attending to pixels to be copied or target lines, showing layer specialization.
Nearby points in task embedding space exhibit similar semantics, suggesting our method learns task relations.
Increasing RE-ARC data increases accuracy, suggesting that common sense about the visual world can be learned from training data.
Increasing training task diversity increases accuracy, suggesting that common sense about the visual world can be learned from training data.
Our method can solve some highly challenging tasks, supporting that visual learning enables abstraction without language.
Our model attempts to interpret ambiguous rules as either 'going-through-only' or 'touching', showing it makes plausible guesses.
Some ARC tasks admit multiple plausible explanations or rules, and our model provides different interpretations.
VARC successfully solves challenging ARC-2 tasks, supporting that visual learning enables abstraction without language.
Layers at different depths tend to focus on different structures, showing Transformer layers specialize in structures/contexts.
Early layers tend to focus on local transformations and context, showing Transformer layers specialize in structures/contexts.
Deep layers are more task-specialized, showing Transformer layers specialize in different structures/contexts.
Some layers exhibit strong attention to 3x3 neighborhoods, while others focus on outward-radiating rays, showing layer specialization.
